headline: Oh, baby: Target makes bold play to win back parents
summary: Target wants to accelerate its comeback by serving a growing, but highly price-competitive market. The retailer, which plans to invest $5 billion in 2026 to remodel existing stores and add news ones, wants to fix a pain point for some of its most stressed out customers. headline: Target fuels next-day delivery expansion with Shipt
summary: The retailer will grow a direct-from-store shipping capability with its subsidiary as it chases lower costs, executives told Supply Chain Dive.

headline: Costco's Comparable Sales Stay Resilient Despite Calendar Headwind
summary: Costco's March comps rise 9.4% despite a lost shopping day, as digital comps jump 23.3% and net sales hit $28.41B.

headline: Walmart Stock Gains 14% YTD: Buy, Hold or Take Profits Now?
summary: WMT shares rise 14% YTD as omnichannel e-commerce, ads and memberships lift profits, but premium valuation and macro risks loom.

raw.headline: Walmart Stock Gains 14% YTD: Buy, Hold or Take Profits Now?

headline: Crisp Expands Minneapolis Presence with New Showcase Office
summary: MINNEAPOLIS, April 09, 2026--Crisp, the leading vertical AI platform for retail, announced the opening of a new showcase office in downtown Minneapolis, underscoring the company’s deep commitment to the Twin Cities’ CPG brands and retailers. The new office, located at the US Bancorp Center, was selected for its central location and offers an open meeting place for partners to utilize while they are in Minneapolis on business.

headline: As millions lose weight on GLP-1s, they're starting to turn to retailers to refresh their wardrobes
summary: Apparel retailers could see an up to $13 billion annual boost in spending because of GLP-1 drugs, according to an estimate by equity research firm Bernstein.
